channel.title: Coffee Terminology & Glossary on Barista At Home
channel.description: Recent content in Coffee Terminology & Glossary on Barista At Home

item.title: Lungo vs Espresso: What's the Difference?
item.description: A lungo uses the same coffee dose as espresso but extracts twice the water through the grounds — producing a larger, more bitter shot. Here's how they compare in flavor, caffeine, and when to choose each.

item.title: Light Roast vs Dark Roast Coffee: Full Comparison (with Medium Roast)
item.description: Light roast vs dark roast: flavor, caffeine, acidity, and which to choose for espresso, drip, and cold brew. Includes medium roast comparison table.

item.title: What Is a Lungo? The Long Espresso Explained
item.description: A lungo is an espresso made with double the water, pulled longer through the grounds for a larger, lighter, slightly more bitter shot. Learn what makes it different from espresso and Americano.

item.title: Espresso Glossary: Every Term You Need to Know
item.description: The complete A-Z reference for espresso and home barista terminology. From yield ratio to crema to tamping pressure — every term defined clearly.
